problem.title: Palindrome Number
problem.description: Given an integer x, return true if x is a palindrome, and false otherwise.

example.explanation: 121 reversed is 121. Negative numbers and numbers ending in 0 (except 0) are not palindromes.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Reverse Half [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Reverse only the second half of the number's digits and compare with the first half, avoiding string conversion entirely. Negative numbers and numbers ending in zero (except zero itself) are immediately false because no such integer can be a palindrome.
